meaning: v.呀呀学语,喋喋不休,作潺潺声,泄露(秘密)n.儿语,胡言乱语,潺潺声
examples: I can't listen to his constant babble.
Kemp knocked loudly so as to be heard above the high babble of voices.
Babies speak baby languages like babble, and they respond to babies in the same baby language.
They were early investors in several digital media properties, including Huffington Post, PaidContent, and Babble.
He would curtail the babble of conflicting statements from governments, which have contributed to the currency's instability.
